intent: Add public-schema support for Required(Any(...)) complex keys so a Schema can require at least one candidate key while still validating whichever fields are present. The merged PR updates voluptuous/schema_builder.py and adds pytest coverage for valid candidate keys, missing-key errors, custom messages, mixed key types, and multiple complex requirements.
notes:
Phase 5.8 case_005 selected by cgpro session phase58-soak (conversation 69ef3a8c-0198-8394-8f09-14a7b120d192) targeting alecthomas/voluptuous@4cef6ce — 'Feature: Support requiring anyOf a list of keys (#534)' (author Miguel Camba). Strategy: target_install=true (editable install required for voluptuous.Schema + Required + Any imports). claim_id=C.voluptuous.required_any_complex_key_capability, claim_type=capability_sufficient (in the verifier's VerifierClaimType Literal), pytest_scope=voluptuous/tests/tests.py.

Pre-dispatch local gate: git clone https://github.com/alecthomas/voluptuous, git checkout 4cef6cee1019741ada6145698e78daa8d73c9353, pip install -e ., pytest voluptuous/tests/tests.py → 167 passed in 0.31s; the 6 new Required(Any(...)) tests (test_required_complex_key_any + test_required_complex_key_custom_message + test_required_complex_key_mixed_types + test_required_complex_key_multiple_complex_requirements + test_required_complex_key_value_validation + test_complex_required_keys_with_specific_value_validation) plus 2 supporting tests all PASS. oida-code verify-grounded --repo-root /tmp/voluptuous-case005 against the bundle → status=verification_candidate, accepted_claims=[C.voluptuous.required_any_complex_key_capability], pytest_summary_line='167 passed in 0.16s', [E.tool.pytest.0].summary='pytest passed scoped to [\'voluptuous/tests/tests.py\'] with no failures (167 passed in 0.16s)'.

Workflow run 25051323517 (success): cross-repo checkout alecthomas/voluptuous@4cef6ce → oida-target/, editable install via inputs.target-install=true, pytest from oida-target/ cwd against scope voluptuous/tests/tests.py → status=ok, [E.tool.pytest.0] kind=test_result with pytest_summary_line='167 passed in 0.17s'. ADR-22 hard wall preserved.

cgpro session phase58-soak (conversation 69ef3a8c-0198-8394-8f09-14a7b120d192) labelled useful_true_positive UX 2/2/2/2 — second case dispatched after the Phase 5.8.x evidence-shape upgrade. cgpro rationale: 'The gateway accepted C.voluptuous.required_any_complex_key_capability as a capability_sufficient claim ... folded pytest evidence now exposes the concrete scoped run result "167 passed in 0.17s" so the operator can see the test file completed cleanly without relying on adapter inference. The pass count alone would not prove the semantic capability, but the combination of the accepted capability_sufficient claim, the targeted audit packet, the scoped voluptuous/tests/tests.py run, and the pre-dispatch confirmation of the eight Required(Any(...)) tests is enough to ground the operator label.'

biggest_trap (cgpro pre-pick): capability is semantic, not just structural — false promotion could happen if the gateway sees the scoped file pass but does not connect the new tests to Required(Any(...)) behavior. The audit stays scoped to pytest evidence and does not treat external CI check status as part of the claim.

Promotion gate: case_005 is the 5th case for aggregator rule 5 (cases_completed>=5 AND usefulness_rate>=0.6 → recommendation=document_opt_in_path). useful_true_positive on case_005 means rule 5 NOW FIRES — the recommendation flips off continue_soak to document_opt_in_path. enable-tool-gateway remains default false in the composite Action regardless.
